evals.prompt: I have a React app with Chameleon components: ch-sidebar with ch-navigation-list-render, ch-tab-render for content tabs, and a form with ch-edit, ch-checkbox, and ch-combo-box-render. I need to add Mercury styling. Show me the getBundles call with all the correct bundles and how to wire ch-theme.
evals.expected_output: Correct getBundles with sidebar, navigation-list, tab, edit, checkbox, combo-box, utils/form. ch-theme as sibling. CSS class 'input' on ch-edit. Label class on labels. field-group and field-block on form containers.

assertions.description: Includes utils/form bundle for labels and field layout
Uses 'label' CSS class on label elements
Uses field-group class for form container
Does NOT include tabular-grid (not needed for ch-edit)
Does NOT include dropdown (only for ch-action-menu-render)
ch-theme placed as sibling, not wrapping children

evals.prompt: I need to set up Mercury in a new Angular project from scratch. The app will use the Globant theme with dark mode support. Walk me through the full installation and configuration.
evals.expected_output: Complete Angular installation with correct imports, mercury CLI (not mercury-build), --globant flag, dark class on html.

assertions.description: Sets dark class on html tag
Does NOT use mercury-build (correct command is mercury)

evals.prompt: I'm building a chat interface with ch-chat that has a code block viewer (ch-code), a markdown response area (ch-markdown-viewer), and action buttons. I need all the Mercury bundles and the correct CSS classes for the buttons (primary action and secondary). What bundles do I need and what classes go on the buttons?
evals.expected_output: Bundles: components/chat, components/markdown-viewer, components/button. No components/code needed (markdown-viewer includes it). No separate send button (ch-chat has one). button-primary and button-secondary classes.

assertions.description: Does NOT include dropdown for regular buttons
ch-theme placed as sibling, not wrapping children

evals.prompt: Build me a settings page with Mercury styling. It should have a page title, a subtitle explaining the page, a form with name, email, and role fields (role is a dropdown), and save/cancel buttons at the bottom. I don't have a Figma design — just vibe code it following Mercury guidelines.
evals.expected_output: Uses heading-1 or heading-2 for title, subtitle-* for description, form with field-group/field-block/label/input classes, ch-edit for inputs, ch-combo-box-render for role dropdown, button-primary for save, button-tertiary for cancel, proper typography classes, Mercury spacing, design tokens for colors.

assertions.description: Uses Mercury heading class (heading-1 or heading-2)
Uses Mercury subtitle class for page description
Uses label class on form labels
Uses ch-combo-box-render for dropdown (not native select)
Does NOT use native select element
Does NOT use native input element
Does NOT use wrong heading-h* class names

evals.prompt: I have a Figma design where text uses font-size 14px with font-weight Regular (400), and a heading at 32px Bold (700). There's a border color #B3B3B3, a background #749519, and text color #3B3B3B. Convert these to Mercury classes and tokens.
evals.expected_output: body-regular-m for 14px text (noting Figma Regular 400 = Mercury Light 300), heading-2 for 32px heading, --color-border-neutral-default for #B3B3B3, --color-accent-primary-default for #749519, --color-text-neutral-default for #3B3B3B. Explains Figma font-weight bug.

assertions.description: Does NOT use wrong heading-h2 class name
Does NOT use raw Figma font-weight value
